title: Dark Room
prompt: Why can we not see in a dark cupboard?

options: No light to reflect into eyes

explanation: We need light sources. Light reflects off objects into our eyes so we see. Darkness is absence of light.
funFact: Cats can see in much dimmer light than us — but not total darkness either!

title: Shadow Size
prompt: Move torch closer to hand puppet — what happens to shadow size?
explanation: Closer light source makes bigger shadow because light is blocked at wider angle.
funFact: Your shadow at noon is shortest because sun is highest!

title: Mirror Maze
explanation: Shiny smooth surfaces reflect well. Dark rough surfaces absorb light.
funFact: The Moon shines because it reflects sunlight — it makes no light of its own!

title: Sun Safety
prompt: What should you never do with bright sunlight?
explanation: Sunlight can damage eyes. Never look directly at sun even with sunglasses.
funFact: Astronauts wear gold visors to protect eyes from harsh sunlight in space!

title: Transparent Test
prompt: Which lets light through clearly so you see sharp image?
explanation: Transparent means clear see-through. Translucent blurry lets some through. Opaque blocks.
funFact: Clean window glass lets about 90 percent of light through!

title: Opaque Block
prompt: Which blocks light completely making dark shadow?
explanation: Opaque materials block light completely. Transparent lets through clearly. Translucent lets some through blurry.
funFact: A brick wall makes a very dark sharp shadow on sunny day!

title: Straight Lines
explanation: Light travels in straight lines until it hits something then reflects or bends or stops.
funFact: You cannot bend light around corner without mirror!

title: Best Reflector
prompt: Which surface reflects best for safety strip on coat at night?
explanation: Shiny smooth light surfaces reflect well. Dark rough absorb.
funFact: Cats eyes shine at night because reflective layer behind retina!

title: Day and Night Why
prompt: Why do we have day and night?

options: Earth spins on its axis once every 24 hours

explanation: Earth rotation causes day night cycle. Day side faces sun, night side faces away.
funFact: At North Pole sun does not set for 6 months in summer!

title: Hot Colours
prompt: Which colour absorbs most light and gets hottest in sun?
explanation: Dark matt surfaces absorb most light turning to heat. White shiny reflect most staying cooler.
funFact: Bedouins wear white robes in desert to reflect heat!
